_meta.changelog: 1.5 (2026-07-14): CRITMIN gains the EV-battery and mineral-supply-chain vocabulary the facet's mineral-name phrases missed — ev battery/batteries, electric vehicle battery/batteries, mineral(s) supply chain, strategic minerals, defense minerals, national defense stockpile, battery minerals, critical material (singular; plural was already in), antimony, rees — proposed by the semantic discovery layer's untagged-neighbor probes (e.g. NanoGraf's 'EV battery component manufacturing', Mosaic's 'battery minerals', US Antimony's defense-processing filings carried no CRITMIN tag in v1.4). 'battery storage' was sampled and REJECTED to display_only (deployment/tax-credit contexts, the ENG beat). Superstring TRAP rule checked (all same-tag). Regression: v1.4's 12,817 CRITMIN filings all retained; other facets byte-identical. 1.4 (2026-07-14): CRYPTO gains the NFT-era vocabulary the facet predated — 'nfts', the four non-fungible-token spelling variants, 'digital collectible(s)', 'digital financial asset(s)' — proposed by the new semantic discovery layer (lda_semantic_search.py untagged-neighbor probe; e.g. Yuga Labs' 'non fungible tokens and digital collectibles' filing and MobileCoin's 'digital financial assets' filings carried no CRYPTO tag in v1.3). Bare 'nft' was caught colliding with the 'New Frontier in Technology (NFT) Act' draft bill in context sampling and rejected to display_only (the 'ripple' rule). No new phrase contains or is contained by an existing phrase (superstring TRAP rule checked). Regression: v1.3's 9,768 CRYPTO filings all retained (set-compared); other facets' tag rows reproduce byte-identically. Semantic discovery also surfaced a Nova Labs filing misspelling 'Crypotcurrency' — reachable by no keyword; left to discovery by design. 1.3 (2026-07-13): added GENIUS facet (the GENIUS Act stablecoin bill, S.1582, carved out of CRYPTO's 'genius act' phrase for its own bill-scoped review package). 'genius act' and the statute's full official name now deliberately map to BOTH the CRYPTO and GENIUS tags — the matcher/build_tags in lda_industry_map.py was generalized from one-tag-per-phrase to a phrase->tags list so this doesn't collide. PARDONS/CRITMIN tag rows reproduce byte-identically on rebuild; CRYPTO's tagged-FILING set is verified IDENTICAL (same 9,768 filings, set-compared) though the raw row count grew 30,922->30,940: 94 rows shifted keyword label (mostly generic 'stablecoins' -> the more specific 'guiding and establishing national innovation for u.s. stablecoins act', the longest-match-wins precision rule already used inside CRYPTO, e.g. 'digital asset market structure' over 'digital asset') and a further 18 gained an additional keyword-citation row on a filing that already carried other CRYPTO keywords — never a lost filing. Two candidate phrases were caught colliding and handled by the discipline's new TRAP rule: 'genius act of 2025' was DROPPED (a pure superstring of 'genius act' with zero added recall, so removing it was strictly safer than promoting it); the long official statute name was PROMOTED into CRYPTO's own phrase list instead of dropped, because it was the sole match for 22 GENIUS-tagged filings and dropping it would have cost real recall. 1.2 (2026-07-11): added CRITMIN facet (critical minerals / battery-materials supply chain, serving ledger L024/BATT), phrases sized + collision-triaged over lobbying_freetext whole-word counts; CRYPTO and PARDONS facets unchanged, so their tag rows reproduce identically on rebuild. 1.1 (2026-07-10): added PARDONS facet (presidential pardons / executive clemency), phrases triaged via lda_freetext_discovery.py --search + whole-word precision counts over lobbying_freetext; crypto facet unchanged, so CRYPTO tag rows reproduce byte-identically on rebuild.
_meta.purpose: Curated industry -> distinctive-phrase vocabulary for the lobbying free-text discovery/serving layer (lda_industry_map.py). Fixes cleanup-note B: an industry hidden under many issue codes and vague categories ('lobbying related to taxation') can only be mapped by the VOCABULARY it uses in the free-text, not by issue-code filtering. Each facet's 'phrases' are matched, whole-word and case-insensitively, against lobbying_freetext (senate activity descriptions + house specific_issues) to build the deterministic, cited serving table lobbying_issue_mentions. Same precision-over-recall discipline as bill_aliases.json.
discipline: Precision over recall, same rule as build_db.py's ISSUE_KEYWORDS and bill_aliases.json. Every 'phrases' string is a distinctive, whole-word-matchable term, never a bare topic word.

'phrases' are the ONLY strings used for tagging. 'display_only' terms are recorded (with the reason) but NOT matched: they are either ambiguous (bare 'token', 'mining', 'wallet', 'coin') or catch unrelated filings (bare 'clarity act' matched an athletic-training filing; the distinctive form 'digital asset market clarity' is used instead).

A facet gets its OWN tag, deliberately NOT folded into an ALI issue code. Crypto scatters across 15+ issue codes (FIN/BAN/TAX/SCI/CPI/CDT/AGR/...), only ~40% under FIN, so the ALI code is not the industry. The tag namespace ('CRYPTO') is separate from the ~79 ALI codes.

The vocabulary is versioned and grows by human triage of the discovery loop (lda_freetext_discovery.py: FTS + keyness + quarterly emergence + untagged mining). Never auto-commit a discovered term; a human adds it here with a source, and the serving table is rebuilt. Bump 'version' and note the change when phrases change so a tagged set stays reproducible.

The industry player list emitted from a facet is entity-resolved through lda-entity-resolver (entities/entity_aliases) so it plugs unchanged into lda_ld203_giving.py --names-file (who they give to) and v_client_canonical_spend (what they spend).

A phrase MAY belong to more than one facet's 'phrases' list when one is a bill-scoped sub-facet of a broader industry (GENIUS is the S.1582 stablecoin bill nested inside CRYPTO's wider vocabulary) — the doc gets a tag row under each matching facet, never a forced either/or choice. This is the only case it should happen; two unrelated facets sharing a phrase is a precision bug, not this pattern.

TRAP: never add a phrase that is a strict superstring of a phrase shared across facets with a DIFFERENT (narrower) tag set — the matcher is longest-match-wins over a single alternation, so the longer phrase steals the match position, and if its tag set doesn't cover the shorter phrase's tags too, those tags silently lose that occurrence. GENIUS v1.3 hit this twice while adding bill-number phrases whose text happens to contain existing CRYPTO vocabulary ('genius act of 2025' contains 'genius act'; the statute's full name contains 'stablecoins') — both caught by a byte-diff regression check against the pre-change CRYPTO row set. Two valid fixes, chosen per-phrase by whether recall would actually be lost: (a) DROP the longer phrase if it adds no recall beyond what the shorter shared phrase already catches ('genius act of 2025' — every occurrence already had 'genius act'); (b) PROMOTE the longer phrase into the union of every tag its substrings carry, so it keeps its own more-specific citation label instead of stealing someone else's tag (the full statute name — 22 filings had no other GENIUS-phrase match, so it was added to CRYPTO's own phrase list too). Never leave it un-fixed: a silent tag loss on a byte-diff-checked facet is exactly the failure this check exists to catch.
_meta.corpus_window: Phrases are calibrated on filings/press 2022-2026 Q1. Bill-name phrases (GENIUS Act, FIT21) are 118th/119th-Congress crypto vehicles; verified distinctive in-window, but re-check when the corpus advances.

facets.label: Cryptocurrency / digital assets
phrases: guiding and establishing national innovation for u.s. stablecoins act

display_only.token: session/tolling/game tokens dominate the bare word; only 'digital token' is kept.
display_only.mining: coal/mineral/data mining (6.7k hits, mostly ALI code NAT); excluded.
display_only.wallet: generic; 'self-hosted wallet' is crypto but low-volume, so wallet is dropped.
display_only.clarity act: bare phrase matched an unrelated athletic-training filing; use 'digital asset market clarity'.
display_only.ripple: 'ripple effect' collision; excluded despite being a crypto firm/coin.
display_only.nft: singular acronym collides with the 'New Frontier in Technology (NFT) Act' draft bill (whole-word context sample, 2026-07-14); 'nfts' and the spelled-out non-fungible-token forms carry the signal safely.

facets.seed_note: The active crypto Enterprise-Map story: an industry hidden under 'taxation' and a dozen other codes, plus diversified filers (Robinhood, PayPal, Fidelity, Stripe, banks) that lobby on crypto without 'crypto' in their name. The vocabulary catches them via what they SAY they lobby on. GENIUS Act (S.1582/S.394, 2025 stablecoin) and FIT21 / the Financial Innovation and Technology for the 21st Century Act (H.R.4763, 118th; market structure) are distinctive crypto bill names and included as phrases; the CLARITY Act appears only via 'digital asset market clarity'. v1.3 (2026-07-13) added the GENIUS Act's full official name ('guiding and establishing national innovation for u.s. stablecoins act') to this list too, alongside the bill-scoped GENIUS facet it seeds — required by the discipline's superstring-tag-union rule (it contains 'stablecoins'), and 22 of its filings had no other GENIUS-facet phrase.
sources: Vocabulary triaged from lda_freetext_discovery.py keyness/emergence over lobbying_freetext, 2026-07-07

NFT/collectibles/digital-financial-assets vocabulary proposed by semantic discovery (lda_semantic_search.py, untagged-neighbor probe) and triaged via whole-word context samples over lobbying_freetext, 2026-07-14 (nfts 17 distinct texts / non-fungible-token forms 15 / digital collectibles 2 / digital financial assets 5; bare 'nft' rejected, see display_only)
facets.confidence: high on the core terms (crypto/digital asset/stablecoin/blockchain); medium on bill-name phrases (window-specific).

facets.label: Presidential pardons / executive clemency

display_only.commute: transportation (commuter rail, clean-commute bills); 76 filings, ~all TRA/ENG; excluded.
display_only.commuted: 0 whole-word hits in-window; transportation risk if it appears; excluded.
display_only.amnesty: immigration amnesty debates + Amnesty International org name; excluded.
display_only.expungement: criminal-RECORDS relief, dominated by cannabis reform (MORE Act, 387 filings); a different legal mechanism than executive clemency; excluded.
display_only.compassionate release: judicial sentence-reduction mechanism (18 U.S.C. 3582), not presidential clemency; 92 filings, sentencing-reform domain; excluded.
display_only.pardon attorney: always co-occurs with the whole word 'pardon', so the phrase adds no filings; recorded for the record.

facets.seed_note: Two distinct populations share this vocabulary and both are in scope: (a) the paid pardon-seeking market — individuals as clients buying 'Executive relief' / 'Seeking a presidential pardon' engagements (ledger L021 Daibes, L034 Ver/Hatch/Schwartz; 'executive relief' whole-phrase resolves to exactly Binance Holdings + Changpeng Zhao + Fred Daibes in-window, a euphemism with near-perfect precision); and (b) clemency-POLICY advocacy by criminal-justice orgs (Due Process Institute, ACLU, marijuana-pardon expansion pushes). The facet deliberately does not split them at tag time; the map's client list separates individuals from orgs downstream.
sources: LEDGER.md L021 (Fred Daibes 'Executive relief' $1M) and L034 (Ver/Hatch/Schwartz paid pardon-seeking, Burkman ~$1.56M) — the leads this facet generalizes

Vocabulary triaged from lda_freetext_discovery.py --search 'pardon' + whole-word precision counts over lobbying_freetext, 2026-07-10 (pardon 152 filings / clemency 170 / executive relief 26; commute/amnesty/expungement/compassionate-release collisions checked and excluded)
facets.confidence: high on pardon/pardons/clemency/executive relief (precision-checked); medium on commutation/commutations (small counts; policy-advocacy contexts verified legit in-window).

facets.label: Critical minerals / battery-materials supply chain

display_only.battery: bare word is the WIDER battery-policy field (EV deployment, storage funding, consumer-battery fires) beyond the materials supply chain — 4,223 filings, incl. 35 artillery/assault-adjacent; the battery-* compound phrases carry the materials signal.
display_only.battery storage: deployment/tax-credit contexts dominate the whole-word sample (solar-plus-storage ITC, behind-the-meter systems, thermal-storage startups) — the ENG deployment beat, not the materials supply chain; 201 distinct texts, only 72 also facet-tagged. Same boundary call as the 'energy storage' medium-confidence warning (2026-07-14).
display_only.lithium-ion: dominated by the Setting Consumer Standards for Lithium-Ion Batteries Act consumer-fire-safety story (CSP 249 of 958 docs) — a different beat than the materials supply chain; 'lithium ion' excluded for the same reason.
display_only.cobalt: Cobalt-60 gamma-sterilization/radiotherapy supply dominates the whole word (HCR/ENV/FOR contexts; Rosatom Sanctions Enforcement Act) — excluded despite being a battery metal (81 filings, most off-facet).
display_only.cathode: 2 whole-word filings in-window — adds nothing; anode carries the electrode signal.
display_only.rare earth elements: subsumed — the whole-word phrase 'rare earth' matches inside it; recorded so its absence isn't read as an omission.
display_only.mining: coal/hardrock/data-mining dominate the bare word (6.7k hits, mostly NAT — same exclusion the crypto facet documented 2026-07-07); the minerals signal arrives via the mineral/materials phrases.

facets.seed_note: Built 2026-07-11 for ledger L024 (BATT Coalition — 'Battery Advocacy for Technology Transformation', ultimate members undisclosed): map the critical-minerals / battery-materials lobbying field BATT files in, so its co-lobby field and the name-invisible players (automakers, miners, chemical and defense filers) become visible. 45X (advanced manufacturing production credit) and 30D (clean vehicle credit) are the IRA credits this field lobbies on; 'foreign entity of concern' is the IRA sourcing restriction; 'lithium' kept after a pharma-collision check found only 4 pharmaceutical-context filings of 1,315; 'nickel' kept after a coin-collision check (Common Cents Act) found only 4 of 255. Copper/aluminum deliberately left untriaged in v1.2 — revisit if the map needs them.
sources: LEDGER.md L024 (BATT Coalition emergence, 2026-07-06) — the lead this facet serves

Vocabulary sized + precision-triaged over lobbying_freetext whole-word counts, 2026-07-11 (critical minerals 4,339 filings / energy storage 2,883 / 45X 1,292 / rare earth 1,311 / 30D 879 / battery manufacturing 726 / critical materials 538 / battery recycling 281 / battery materials 264 / graphite 226 / battery supply chain 171 / foreign entity of concern 133 / anode 74; collision samples for battery/lithium/nickel/cobalt/lithium-ion recorded in display_only)

EV-battery / mineral-supply-chain / stockpile vocabulary proposed by semantic discovery (lda_semantic_search.py untagged-neighbor probes) and triaged via whole-word context samples, 2026-07-14 (ev battery 97 distinct texts / mineral supply chain 84 / critical material singular 74 / electric vehicle batteries 69 / electric vehicle battery 52 / national defense stockpile 40 / minerals supply chain 15 / strategic minerals 14 / defense minerals 14 / antimony 7 / ev batteries 7 / rees 4 all-clean / battery minerals 3; 'battery storage' rejected, see display_only)
facets.confidence: high on the mineral/materials phrases and the statutory terms (45X/30D/foreign entity of concern); medium on energy storage (includes non-battery storage: hydrogen, pumped hydro) and advanced batteries (36 filings).

facets.label: GENIUS Act (stablecoin regulatory framework, S.1582)
phrases: guiding and establishing national innovation for u.s. stablecoins act

display_only.stablecoin legislation: generic — could name the GENIUS Act, the CLARITY Act market-structure bill, or a state-level proposal; 366 whole-word docs, not GENIUS-distinctive; excluded.
display_only.stablecoin regulation: same ambiguity as 'stablecoin legislation' (99 docs); excluded.
display_only.stablecoin bill: same ambiguity, lower volume (22 docs); excluded.
display_only.1582: bare bill number collides with section/page references outside a bill-number context; only the 'S. 1582' / 'S.1582' forms are distinctive.

facets.seed_note: Carved out of the CRYPTO facet's 'genius act' phrase (present since lexicon v1.0) as its own bill-scoped facet so the GENIUS Act — the Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act, S.1582, 119th Congress, signed into law July 2025, the single most distinctive crypto legislative vehicle in-window — supports its own review package rather than only living inside the broader crypto map. 'genius act' deliberately maps to BOTH the CRYPTO and GENIUS tags (see _meta.discipline); this facet adds the bill-number citation forms (S. 1582 / S.1582 / the statute's full name) that lift recall beyond the bare popular name. NOTE: 'genius act of 2025' was tried and DROPPED — as a strict superstring of the shared phrase 'genius act', longest-match-wins made it steal the match position at every occurrence, which (since it mapped to GENIUS only) silently dropped the CRYPTO tag for those spans — a 413-row/41-filing CRYPTO regression caught by the byte-diff regression check and reverted same day. 'genius act' alone already has full recall for 'GENIUS Act of 2025' mentions. The full statute name phrase hit the same trap (it contains CRYPTO's 'stablecoins') but 22 of its filings were GENIUS-only, so it was PROMOTED into CRYPTO's own phrase list instead (see CRYPTO facet seed_note) rather than dropped. The lesson is recorded in _meta.discipline.
sources: Vocabulary triaged by whole-word LIKE counts over lobbying_freetext, 2026-07-13 (genius act 1,066 docs; s. 1582 293 rows / s.1582 157 rows before de-dup; full statute name 72; combined recall ~1,178 distinct docs after the superstring-collision fix); ambiguous generic terms spot-checked and recorded display_only.
facets.confidence: high — every phrase form spot-checked against raw free-text context; ambiguous generic terms (stablecoin legislation/regulation/bill) deliberately excluded as display_only rather than risk pulling in CLARITY Act or state-level stablecoin coverage.
